FluentGpu
Table of Contents

Class ImageCache

Namespace
FluentGpu.Scene
Assembly
FluentGpu.Scene.dll

Portable image residency: source→handle dedup, a Pending/Ready/Failed state machine, liveness ref-counting (a pinned/on-screen image is never evicted — the single biggest real-world cache bug, per the research), and an LRU byte budget over decoded images. Decode happens off-thread behind IImageDecoder; Pump() applies completions (+1-frame latency). The GPU texture upload is a needs-pixels leaf concern; this owns the logic.

public sealed class ImageCache

object
ImageCache

ImageCache(IImageDecoder, long)

Parameters

decoder IImageDecoder
budgetBytes long

Properties

Count 

public int Count { get; }

Property Value

int

HasActiveCrossfades

True while any image is still revealing — the host keeps painting so the fade animates to completion. O(1): equivalent to the former per-frame scan (∃ enabled reveal with clockMs < TextureMs+Dur) because that is exactly (_clockMs < max deadline). See FluentGpu.Scene.ImageCache._maxCrossfadeDeadlineMs (wake-04).

public bool HasActiveCrossfades { get; }

Property Value

bool

PendingCount

Entries still decoding (State==Pending) — O(1) maintained counter (was a per-call scan, wake-04).

public int PendingCount { get; }

Property Value

int

ReadyCount 

public int ReadyCount { get; }

Property Value

int

UsedBytes 

public long UsedBytes { get; }

Property Value

long

Methods

AttemptsOf(ImageHandle)

How many fetch attempts the decoder made (≥1 once resolved; >1 means transient retries happened).

public int AttemptsOf(ImageHandle h)

Parameters

h ImageHandle

Returns

int

BucketFor(int)

Round a display size up to a decode bucket (64/128/256/512) — the texture-pool / atlas granularity.

public static int BucketFor(int px)

Parameters

px int

Returns

int

Cancel(ImageHandle)

Cancel an in-flight decode (row recycled / unmounted) — frees worker + network effort under fast scroll.

public void Cancel(ImageHandle h)

Parameters

h ImageHandle

CrossFadeOf(ImageHandle)

Placeholder→image reveal progress 0..1, eased by the image's ImageTransition from when its first texture appeared (the BlurHash LQIP, or the full-res decode). 1 once settled, instantly if the transition is disabled, or if there's no texture yet.

public float CrossFadeOf(ImageHandle h)

Parameters

h ImageHandle

Returns

float

FailureOf(ImageHandle)

Why an image is Failed (None otherwise) — for app fallbacks / retry UI.

public ImageFailureKind FailureOf(ImageHandle h)

Parameters

h ImageHandle

Returns

ImageFailureKind

Pin(ImageHandle)

Pin = "on screen" (a realized node holds it); never evicted while pinned. Unpin on recycle/unmount.

public void Pin(ImageHandle h)

Parameters

h ImageHandle

Prefetch(string, int, int)

Warm the cache for a source the UI is ABOUT to need (the next scroll page) at Prefetch — decoded ahead, never pinned, so it's instantly Resident when a row scrolls in (Nuke/Coil prefetch). Evictable until a real node pins it. No-op if already requested.

public ImageHandle Prefetch(string source, int targetW, int targetH)

Parameters

source string
targetW int
targetH int

Returns

ImageHandle

Pump()

Apply finished decodes (UI thread, once per frame) then evict to budget. Returns completions this pump. Allocation-free when idle (cached callback; empty-queue Pump does nothing) — safe in the hot phase.

public int Pump()

Returns

int

RefsOf(ImageHandle) 

public int RefsOf(ImageHandle h)

Parameters

h ImageHandle

Returns

int

Request(string, int, int, ImagePriority, string?, ImageTransition?)

Request (or dedup) a decode of source at a target size; returns a stable handle. On a cache MISS, an optional blurHash is decoded ONCE into a tiny LQIP texture (uploaded under this id) so a blurred preview shows instantly until the full-res art lands. A cache HIT returns before any of this — so re-renders of the same image do no work.

public ImageHandle Request(string source, int targetW, int targetH, ImagePriority priority = ImagePriority.Visible, string? blurHash = null, ImageTransition? transition = null)

Parameters

source string
targetW int
targetH int
priority ImagePriority
blurHash string
transition ImageTransition?

Returns

ImageHandle

SetEvictSink(Action<int>)

The host wires this to IGpuDevice.EvictImage; the cache calls it when residency evicts an image so the backend frees the GPU texture. Set once at composition.

public void SetEvictSink(Action<int> sink)

Parameters

sink Action<int>

SetPixelSink(ImageReadyHandler)

The host wires this to IGpuDevice.UploadImage; the cache forwards each decode's pixels through it during Pump() (transiently — the sink must copy, not store). Set once at composition.

public void SetPixelSink(ImageReadyHandler sink)

Parameters

sink ImageReadyHandler

h ImageHandle

(int Start, int End)

h ImageHandle

ImageState

Tick(float)

Advance the cross-fade clock by dtMs (call once per painted frame, before record).

public void Tick(float dtMs)

Parameters

dtMs float

Unpin(ImageHandle) 

public void Unpin(ImageHandle h)

Parameters

h ImageHandle

Events

ImageStatusChanged

Raised (UI thread, during Pump()) when an image reaches a terminal state — Ready or Failed. Apps subscribe for a broken-art fallback, a retry/offline toast, or telemetry. See also FailureOf(ImageHandle).

public event ImageStatusHandler? ImageStatusChanged

Event Type

ImageStatusHandler
FluentGpu — a near-zero-allocation, NativeAOT, GPU-rendered UI engine for .NET 10. Usage docs live here; architecture canon lives in design/ (SPEC-INDEX.md is the precedence authority).
